articles.title: From Superstar To 'Flop Actor': Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle Trailer Sparks Buzz
articles.description: The trailer of Welcome to the Jungle is finally here, and it's bringing exactly what fans expected—absolute chaos. Starring Akshay Kumar as a struggling "flop actor" caught in a fake ₹2000-crore film that spirals into real danger, the trailer embraces self-aware humour, over-the-top comedy, and classic Welcome-style madness. With a massive ensemble cast including Jackie Shroff, Suniel Shetty, Paresh Rawal and many more, the film looks set to deliver confusion, comedy and complete entertainment. Film fake, problems real—and the internet can't stop talking about it.

articles.title: Dwayne Johnson recalls frightening testicular cancer scare before Jumanji press event: 'I had to live with that for those 24 hour'
articles.description: Dwayne Johnson has revealed that he faced a cancer scare earlier this year after discovering a lump on one of his testicles just before a major Jumanji promotional event. The actor said doctors initially warned it could be cancer.

articles.title: Bitcoin (BTC USD) price drops toward the $60,000 level as two major buyers pull back - what's happening with the cryptocurrency?
articles.description: Bitcoin market crash 2026​ analysis: Bitcoin's price decline is attributed to a simultaneous slowdown in demand from two key sources: spot ETFs and digital asset treasury firms. Spot ETFs have seen significant net outflows totaling over $5.72 billion since mid-May, while treasury firms have sharply reduced their buying pace, shifting from aggressive accumulation to caution.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Brad Pitt: 'Everything is more beautiful because we’re doomed...' - A life lesson about appreciating the present moment by the Fight Club star
articles.description: Hollywood star Brad Pitt's quote, "Everything is more beautiful because we’re doomed. You will never be more lovely than you are now. We will never be here again," emphasizes the preciousness of the present. His words encourage appreciating life's fleeting moments, as their impermanence is what imbues them with beauty and meaning, urging us to live fully in the now.

articles.title: Beijing's trolls India over NEET: Gaokao didn't leak, but other exams in China did
articles.description: China's embassy in India highlighted the smooth conduct of its massive Gaokao exam, contrasting it with India's exam leak issues. While Gaokao faces fewer large-scale leaks, past incidents of cheating and alleged leaks in other Chinese exams, like the postgraduate entrance, have occurred. The Gaokao remains crucial for Chinese students' futures

articles.title: Vietnamese proverb of the day: 'The tree wishes for calm, but...' Life lessons on staying strong when life refuses to get easier
articles.description: Vietnamese proverb of the day: Life presents constant challenges, much like a tree facing an unceasing wind. The Vietnamese proverb "The tree wishes for calm, but the wind will not stop" teaches acceptance of uncontrollable circumstances. It highlights that strength develops by facing difficulties, not avoiding them. Individuals learn to move forward despite uncertainty, focusing on their reactions to challenges.

articles.title: Haryana guest teacher suspended days after joining Cockroach Janta Party protest in Delhi
articles.description: A guest teacher in Haryana's Rohtak district has been suspended after participating in a protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. Sulekha Dalal's suspension, effective June 8, followed a viral video of her addressing the Cockroach Janta Party rally. Dalal stated her participation was due to concerns over recruitment exam irregularities affecting her son.

articles.title: TS Inter supplementary exam 2026: Not even half of second year general streams students passed exam, check details here
articles.description: The Telangana Intermediate Supplementary Results 2026 revealed a worrying trend in the second-year General stream, where the pass percentage remained below 50 per cent despite students getting another opportunity to clear subjects. While first-year candidates showed improvement and girls continued to outperform boys, the results highlighted persistent academic challenges among second-year students.

articles.title: 'She uncovered his affair, secret Rs 2.5-cr loan': Punjab doctor 'who helped fund' husband's eye hospital found dead
articles.description: A doctor's husband, an eye surgeon, faces charges of abetting suicide after his wife was found dead. Her parents allege extramarital affairs, violence, and a massive Rs 2.5 crore loan taken in her name without consent. The couple had been living separately following alleged assault attempts and threats.
